mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and confidence, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 are negative or flat (less than 0.1% average) during periods of consistent RSAFS growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, evidenced by the unemployment rate falling below a critical threshold, signals strong economic health and increasing wage growth, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns.
failure_condition: If, after UNRATE falls below 5.0%, the average S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative over the subsequent 12 months in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is actively raising interest rates in an environment of elevated inflation, it signals a restrictive monetary policy aimed at cooling the economy, which increases the cost of capital and reduces future corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 3.0%
failure_condition: If, when the trigger conditions are met, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month increase in housing starts during a period of economic recovery indicates renewed consumer and business confidence, signaling a broader pickup in economic activity and investment, which drives positive short-term equity market sentiment.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances during identified post-recession recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real Gross Domestic Product signifies an expanding economy with increasing aggregate demand, which directly supports higher corporate earnings and valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the correlation between GDPC1 growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns turns negative or insignificant (p-value > 0.1) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in industrial production and payroll employment indicates a robust and balanced economic expansion where both supply and demand are healthy, leading to sustained corporate profitability and positive equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month AND PAYEMS rises > 100k month-over-month
failure_condition: If, when both INDPRO and PAYEMS triggers are met, the average S&P 500 60-day forward return is less than 0.5% over the subsequent 12 months in more than 60% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and significant increase in the federal funds rate signals an aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, which can shock financial markets by increasing borrowing costs and reducing liquidity, leading to immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points in a single month.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, which typically leads to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate profits, and consequently, negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, when UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.
